ticketAnalysis: Task E3-S2-T1 requires implementing C++ button click handler functions for a Qt 6.10 calculator desktop application. Each calculator button (digits 0-9, arithmetic operators +, -, *, /, equals, clear, decimal point, etc.) must have a dedicated slot function wired via Qt's signal-slot mechanism to respond to QPushButton clicked() signals. The handlers must dispatch the correct arithmetic operation or input command to the application logic, with proper input validation (e.g., division by zero guard). The existing file app/calc_operations.cpp is the designated extension point.
orientationSummary: The repository is extremely minimal — only one file exists in the app/ directory (app/calc_operations.cpp) as declared in the wiring. Prior tasks (E3-S1-T1 through E3-S1-T3) created a Qt UI layout and integrated it into the main window with responsive design. No header files or main window class files are explicitly listed in the repo map (totalFiles: 1), but calc_operations.cpp is confirmed as the file to extend. Given the wiring and architecture, companion header files (app/calc_operations.h) and a main window class (app/mainwindow.h / app/mainwindow.cpp) are necessary to implement signal-slot connections properly, and a test file is required by the test requirements.
implementationOrder: 1. Read app/calc_operations.cpp and REPO_MAP.md to understand existing structure and any declared symbols

2. Create app/calc_operations.h — declare CalcOperations class with slots for each button: onDigitPressed(int digit), onAddPressed(), onSubtractPressed(), onMultiplyPressed(), onDividePressed(), onEqualsPressed(), onClearPressed(), onDecimalPressed(), onBackspacePressed(); also declare signals: commandIssued(QString cmd)

3. Extend app/calc_operations.cpp — implement all slot functions from the header, managing internal state (current operand, pending operator, accumulator), handling division-by-zero with a user-friendly error string, and emitting commandIssued signal where appropriate

4. Create app/mainwindow.h — declare MainWindow class (QMainWindow subclass) with private CalcOperations* m_ops and private slot connectButtons(); declare references to all QPushButton members matching the UI layout

5. Create app/mainwindow.cpp — implement MainWindow constructor that instantiates CalcOperations, calls connectButtons(), and in connectButtons() uses QObject::connect() to wire each button's clicked() signal to the corresponding CalcOperations slot using Qt6 functor syntax

6. Create tests/test_button_handlers.cpp — implement unit and integration tests using QTest framework (Qt-compatible): test each slot emits/processes the correct command, test signal-slot connections are established, test edge cases (division by zero, consecutive operators)

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.criterion: AC1: All buttons are wired to corresponding arithmetic operation functions.
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.step: Steps 4 & 5: MainWindow::connectButtons() uses QObject::connect() to wire every QPushButton (digits 0-9, +, -, *, /, =, C, ., backspace) clicked() signal to the matching CalcOperations slot, fully implemented in app/mainwindow.cpp

outOfScope: Do not modify or create CMakeLists.txt or .pro build files beyond what is automatically handled
Do not introduce a separate server directory or non-Qt frameworks
Do not implement UI layout changes — that was completed in E3-S1-T1 through E3-S1-T3
Do not implement scientific or advanced calculator functions beyond core arithmetic (+, -, *, /, =, C, decimal, backspace, digits)
Do not add cloud, authentication, or database functionality
Do not duplicate existing layout/navigation modules from prior sprint tasks
rationale: app/calc_operations.cpp is the mandated wiring target and must be extended with all handler implementations. app/calc_operations.h is required as the companion header declaring the CalcOperations QObject subclass with Q_OBJECT macro so Qt's moc can process signals/slots — without it, the .cpp cannot compile as a Qt class. app/mainwindow.h and app/mainwindow.cpp are necessary to house the Qt signal-slot connect() calls that wire buttons to handlers (AC1 explicitly requires all buttons to be wired); the prior sprint tasks created the UI layout which implies a MainWindow class exists or must be created. tests/test_button_handlers.cpp satisfies the unit and integration test requirements stated in the task. All new files reside within the app/ or tests/ directories consistent with the monolithic Qt desktop app structure.
